meta.title: Synthetic Data Generation and Fine tuning (OpenAI GPT4o or Llama 3)
meta.description:
➡️ Get Life-time Access to the Complete Scripts (and future improvements): https://Trelis.com/ADVANCED-fine-tuning
➡️ One-click fine-tuning and LLM templates: https://github.com/TrelisResearch/one-click-llms
➡️ Newsletter: https://blog.Trelis.com
➡️ Resources/Support/Discord: https://Trelis.com/About
➡️ Thumbnail made with this tutorial: https://youtu.be/ThKYjTdkyP8

VIDEO RESOURCES:
- Slides: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/148ViyUY82IlnIaJDrv2Daon-Gb0VaweVKctl5Me7w-o/edit?usp=sharing
- Touch Rugby Synthetic Data: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Trelis/touch-rugby-synth
- lighteval/MATH: https://huggingface.co/datasets/lighteval/MATH

TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 How to generate synthetic data for fine-tuning
0:47 Video Overview (fine-tune OpenAI or Llama 3)
2:20: Synthetic Question Generation
5:48 Synthetic Answer Generation
8:00 Why chain of thought is important in Synthetic Data
10:06 Augmented Synthetic Data
11:15 Generating Synthetic Data from Documents
13:35 Synthetic  Data from Structured Data
14:33 Generating data from user conversations
16:56 GPU and notebook setup for Notebooks
19:21 OpenAI Notebook: Data Generation and Fine-tuning
23:15 Data extraction from pdfs
25:00 Synthetic Data Generation for GPT-4o-mini fine-tuning
26:25 Generating synthetic questions using structured outputs
27:35 Generating synthetic answers
28:39 Saving data in jsonl format for OpenAI fine-tuning
31:47 How to fine-tune an openai model on a synthetic dataset
34:45 Using an LLM as a judge for evaluation
37:31 Evaluation of gpt-4o-mini versus fine-tuned model
39:19 How to increase and improve the training data
41:40 Fine-tuning Open Source Models like Llama 3
44:13 Pushing a synthetic dataset to HuggingFace
46:12 Loading a model with transformers or Unsloth
47:25 Setting generation parameters incl. temperature and top p
49:10 Batch generation with transformers or unsloth, incl. padding and chat templating
51:12 Llama 3.2 8B model performance (before fine-tuning)
53:00 Fine-tuning on synthetic data with unsloth or transformers
53:17 LoRA adapter setup, rescaled LoRa, choice of rank and alpha
56:01 Dataset preparation for fine-tuning, incl. prompt formatting
57:34 SFTTrainer (trainer) setup incl. epochs, batch size, gradient accumulation
58:25 Defining a custom learning schedule with annealing
1:01:31 How to train on completions only (like openai’s default)
1:02:32 Running training on Llama 3.2 1B
1:03:31 Performance evaluation after fine-tuning Llama 3.2
1:07:24 Using augmented synthetic data to improve Maths performance (Advanced / Speculative!)
1:07:11 Evaluating the baseline maths performance of Llama 3.2 1B
1:08:58 Fine-tuning on a training split of the lighteval/MATH dataset
1:09:13 Training on synthetic data from Llama 3.1 8B (instead of the training split)
1:12:21 Comparing results of training on a training split vs on synthetic Llama 3.1 8B answers
1:13:14 Training on an augmented synthetic dataset generated with Llama 3.1 8B and ground truth answers
1:16:08 Comparing all results, base vs fine-tuned on the raw dataset vs 8B synth vs 8B synth with augmentation
1:16:30 How to use augmented data if you have access to user conversations or feedback
